findings.message: No robots.txt found. Agents can access all content by default.
Consider adding a robots.txt that explicitly permits AI agent crawlers.
findings.fix:
Create a robots.txt with:
User-agent: ClaudeBot
Allow: /

User-agent: GPTBot
Allow: /

findings.message: No llms.txt found at site root.
findings.fix:
Create a llms.txt file at your site root. This acts as a sitemap for AI agents.
See: https://llmstxt.org for the specification.

findings.message: No AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, or similar agent instruction file found.
findings.fix:
Create an AGENTS.md in your repo root. This is the entry point for AI coding agents.
Include: project structure, key file locations, API docs links, dev environment setup, and coding conventions.

findings.message: 9 of 10 sampled pages have broken heading hierarchy.
findings.fix: Fix heading levels to be sequential (H1 → H2 → H3). This is critical for agent parsing.

findings.message: Few pages (0/10) contain code examples.
findings.fix: Code examples are critical for agents. Add runnable examples for key operations.

findings.message: 0 of 10 pages use tables.
findings.fix: Consider using tables for parameter references and comparison data. Tables compress better than prose lists for agents.

findings.message: Good use of semantic HTML elements (main, article, section).
Many pages lack a strong opening paragraph.
findings.fix: Start each page with a clear statement of what the page covers and what the reader/agent will learn.

findings.message: No Markdown files found. Only HTML is available.
findings.fix:
Serve Markdown versions of documentation pages. Many static site generators can output .md files alongside .html.
Consider adding a ?format=md query parameter or .md URL suffix support.

findings.message: HTML pages have good content-to-markup ratio.
Content is accessible without JavaScript rendering.

token-economics.name: Token Economics
token-economics.description: Does content fit within agent context windows?

findings.message: Average page size: 2,264 tokens (excellent).
No pages exceed 50K tokens.
100% of pages are under 30K tokens.
Analyzed 10 pages. Total: 22,644 tokens.
No pages include token count meta tags.
findings.fix:
Add <meta name="ai:token-count" content="12500"> to help agents estimate context cost.
Alternatively, expose token count as an X-Token-Count HTTP header.

findings.message: 10 of 10 pages have meaningful meta descriptions.
2 of 10 pages have page type metadata.
findings.fix: Consider adding <meta name="ai:page-type" content="api-reference|guide|tutorial|quickstart"> to categorize pages for agents.

findings.message: Pages have clean, descriptive title tags.
6 of 10 pages have canonical URLs.

capability-signaling.name: Capability Signaling
capability-signaling.description: Can agents understand what your APIs do?

findings.message: No skill.md files found.
findings.fix:
Create skill.md files for your APIs and services. These tell agents what your product can DO, not just how to call it.
Include: capabilities list, required inputs, constraints, and links to detailed docs.

findings.message: No agent-permissions.json found.
findings.fix:
Consider creating an agent-permissions.json to define access rules for automated clients.
This emerging standard lets you specify allowed interactions, rate limits, and human-in-the-loop requirements.

ux-bridge.name: UX Bridge
ux-bridge.description: Does the UX support human-agent workflows?

findings.message: No "Copy for AI" buttons found.
findings.fix:
Add a "Copy for AI" button that copies clean Markdown or plain text to clipboard.
This bridges human and agent workflows when developers work inside AI assistants.

findings.message: No copy-to-clipboard buttons found for code blocks.
findings.fix: Add copy buttons to code blocks. This is standard in modern documentation and helps both human and agent workflows.

findings.message: No links to raw or Markdown views found.
findings.fix: Add links to Markdown source or "Edit on GitHub" links. These help agents and developers access clean, parseable content.
